2025-01-13 13:49:43,719 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 13:49:43,719 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options. 
4. Fill out the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 13:49:48,057 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in bdd format. 
2025-01-13 13:49:48,057 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Feature: Account Balances and Transaction History Viewing 

Scenario: Successfully view account balances and transaction history 
Given a customer has a valid bank account 
When the customer accesses their account and requests to view their balances and transaction history 
Then the correct and up-to-date account balances, along with the detailed transaction history, are shown to the customer. 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 13:49:52,880 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 13:49:52,880 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Log in to the banking website with your customer credentials. 
2. Navigate to the "Accounts" page. 
3. Verify that all of your accounts are listed correctly. 
4. Select the account from which you want to transfer money. 
5. Click on the "Transfer Money" option. 
6. Select the account to which you want to transfer money. 
7. Enter the amount of money you want to transfer. 
8. Click the "Submit" button. 
9. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the transfer was successful. 
10. Verify that the new balance of the source account has been reduced by the transfer amount. 
11. Verify that the new balance of the destination account has been increased by the transfer amount. 
12. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 13:49:55,786 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Feature: Online Bill Payment 

Scenario: Successfully pay bills online 
Given a customer has a valid bank account  
And the customer has access to the online banking system 
When the customer accesses their account and requests to pay a bill online 
Then the payment should be processed correctly  
And the account balance should be updated accordingly 
And a confirmation of the payment should be shown to the customer. 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 13:49:55,909 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': '1. Access account balance page.\\n2. Verify the displayed balance matches the database value.,', 'format': 'plain_text', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 0.29
2025-01-13 13:50:04,498 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 13:50:04,499 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Completion: Test Case: 
1. Log in to the banking website with your customer credentials. 
2. Navigate to the "Account Alerts" or "Notification Settings" page. 
3. Verify that options for setting up different types of alerts are available (e.g., balance alerts, transaction alerts). 
4. Select the type of alert you want to set up. 
5. Set the conditions for the alert (e.g., balance below a certain amount, transaction over a certain amount). 
6. Choose the method of notification (email, text message, push notification). 
7. Click the "Save" or "Set Alert" button. 
8. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the alert has been successfully set up. 
9. Test the alert by triggering the set condition (e.g., make a transaction that would trigger the alert, or adjust your balance to trigger a balance alert). 
10. Verify that the alert notification is received by the chosen method. 
11. Check that the alert details in the notification match the setup conditions. 
12. Log out from the account.

Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the transfer was successful.\n10. Verify that the new balance of the source account has been reduced by the transfer amount.\n11. Verify that the new balance of the destination account has been increased by the transfer amount.\n12. Log out from the account.', 'format': 'plain_text', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 0.50
2025-01-13 14:05:38,448 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 14:05:38,448 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options given. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 14:05:44,535 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Feature: Account Balances and Transaction History Viewing 

Scenario: Successfully view account balances and transaction history 
Given a customer has a valid bank account 
When the customer accesses their account and requests to view their balances and transaction history 
Then the correct and up-to-date account balances, along with the detailed transaction history, are shown to the customer. 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 16:04:47,028 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 16:04:47,028 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the options. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

Scenario: Successfully view account balances and transaction history 
Given a customer has a valid bank account 
When the customer accesses their account and requests to view their balances and transaction history 
Then the correct and up-to-date account balances, along with the detailed transaction history, are shown to the customer. 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 18:00:54,586 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': 'Test Case:\n1. Log in to the banking website with your customer credentials.\n2. Navigate to the "Accounts" page.\n3. Verify that all of your accounts are listed correctly.\n4. Select the account from which you want to transfer money.\n5. Click on the "Transfer Money" option.\n6. Select the account to which you want to transfer money.\n7. Enter the amount of money you want to transfer.\n8. Click the "Submit" button.\n9. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the transfer was successful.\n10. Verify that the new balance of the source account has been reduced by the transfer amount.\n11. Verify that the new balance of the destination account has been increased by the transfer amount.\n12. Log out from the account.', 'format': 'plain_text', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 0.50
2025-01-13 18:01:04,782 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 18:01:04,782 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button. 
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 18:09:06,002 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 18:09:06,002 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options provided. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account using the provided credentials. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account using the provided credentials. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 18:27:17,150 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 18:27:17,150 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Open the web browser and navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Locate and click on the "Open New Account" button on the homepage. 
3. From the options provided, select "Checking Account". 
4. A form will appear that requires your personal information. Fill in your name, address, social security number, and the amount you wish to deposit initially. 
5. After filling out the information, click the "Submit" button. 
6. A confirmation message should appear on the screen stating that your checking account has been successfully opened. Verify this. 
7. Log in to the newly created account using your credentials. 
8. Check the account balance and verify that it reflects the initial deposit amount you inputted. 
9. Check the account details including your name, address and other information. Make sure they are correctly displayed. 
10. After verifying all the details, log out from the account.

Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount.\n9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed.\n10. Log out from the account.', 'format': 'plain_text', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 1.00

2025-01-13 18:55:20,632 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 18:55:20,632 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the list of options. 
4. Fill in the required information such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account using the provided credentials. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account. 
2025-01-13 18:55:20,632 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Tokenized into 1 chunks.

2025-01-13 18:56:05,311 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in plain_text format. 
2025-01-13 18:56:05,311 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Test Case: 
1. Navigate to the bank's website. 
2. Click on the "Open New Account" button.  
3. Select "Checking Account" from the available options. 
4. Fill in the required fields such as name, address, social security number, and initial deposit amount. 
5. Click the "Submit" button. 
6. Verify that a confirmation message appears stating the checking account has been successfully opened. 
7. Log in to the new account using the provided credentials. 
8. Verify that the account balance reflects the initial deposit amount. 
9. Verify that the account details (name, address etc.) are correctly displayed. 
10. Log out from the account.

2025-01-13 18:58:34,634 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in bdd format. 
2025-01-13 18:58:34,635 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening 

Scenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
And they submit the application form 
Then they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress 
And they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in incomplete personal and financial information 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed 
And the application form should not be submitted 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
But they have a poor internet connection 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues 
And they should be prompted to try again later 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 21:23:02,441 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': "Feature: Generated Test Case\n\nScenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening\n\nScenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nAnd they submit the application form\nThen they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress\nAnd they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in incomplete personal and financial information\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed\nAnd the application form should not be submitted\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nBut they have a poor internet connection\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues\nAnd they should be prompted to try again later\nGiven precondition\nWhen action\nThen expected result", 'format': 'bdd', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 1.00
2025-01-13 21:23:02,441 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': "Feature: Generated Test Case\n\nScenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening\n\nScenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nAnd they submit the application form\nThen they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress\nAnd they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in incomplete personal and financial information\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed\nAnd the application form should not be submitted\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nBut they have a poor internet connection\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues\nAnd they should be prompted to try again later\nGiven precondition\nWhen action\nThen expected result", 'format': 'bdd', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 1.00
2025-01-13 21:23:11,658 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in bdd format. 
2025-01-13 21:23:11,660 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening 

Scenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
And they submit the application form 
Then they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress 
And they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in incomplete personal and financial information 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed 
And the application form should not be submitted 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
But they have a poor internet connection 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues 
And they should be prompted to try again later. 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

Scenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening 

Scenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
And they submit the application form 
Then they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress 
And they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in incomplete personal and financial information 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed 
And the application form should not be submitted 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
But they have a poor internet connection 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues 
And they should be prompted to try again later 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result

2025-01-13 21:33:56,788 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': "Feature: Generated Test Case\n\nScenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening\n\nScenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nAnd they submit the application form\nThen they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress\nAnd they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in incomplete personal and financial information\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed\nAnd the application form should not be submitted\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nBut they have a poor internet connection\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues\nAnd they should be prompted to try again later\nGiven precondition\nWhen action\nThen expected result", 'format': 'bdd', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 1.00
2025-01-13 21:33:56,788 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Document Metadata: {'completion': "Feature: Generated Test Case\n\nScenario: Feature: Online Checking Account Opening\n\nScenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nAnd they submit the application form\nThen they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress\nAnd they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in incomplete personal and financial information\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed\nAnd the application form should not be submitted\n\nScenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection\nGiven a new customer is on the bank's website\nWhen they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'\nAnd they fill in the required personal and financial information\nBut they have a poor internet connection\nAnd they attempt to submit the application form\nThen they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues\nAnd they should be prompted to try again later\nGiven precondition\nWhen action\nThen expected result", 'format': 'bdd', 'type': 'requirement'}, Similarity Score: 1.00
2025-01-13 21:34:04,895 - GenAIPOT - INFO - Generated test case in bdd format. 
2025-01-13 21:34:04,895 - GenAIPOT - DEBUG - Generated Test Case: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Scenario: Completion: Feature: Generated Test Case 

Feature: Online Checking Account Opening 

Scenario: Successful online opening of a new checking account 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
And they submit the application form 
Then they should receive a confirmation message that the account opening is in progress 
And they should receive an email with instructions on how to set up online banking 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to insufficient information 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in incomplete personal and financial information 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see an error message highlighting the fields that need to be completed 
And the application form should not be submitted 

Scenario: Unsuccessful online opening of a new checking account due to poor internet connection 
Given a new customer is on the bank's website 
When they navigate to 'Open a New Checking Account'
And they fill in the required personal and financial information 
But they have a poor internet connection 
And they attempt to submit the application form 
Then they should see a message indicating that the submission was unsuccessful due to connectivity issues 
And they should be prompted to try again later 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result 
Given precondition 
When action 
Then expected result
